title: Could not resolve host with podman-compose
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->
After installing podman via brew, running a podman-compose where one container attempts to resolve the other fails with `Could not resolve host`.  

**compose.yaml**
```
version: '3.1'

services:
  nginx:
    image: nginx
  client:
    image: alpine
    command: "sh -c 'apk add curl && curl -v nginx:80 && echo It works'"
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. brew update
2. brew install podman 
3. mkdir ~/podman
4. podman machine init --cpus 2 --memory 4096 -v ~/podman:/mnt/podman
5. podman machine start
6. sudo /opt/homebrew/Cellar/podman/4.1.0/bin/podman-mac-helper install
7. podman-compose -f compose.yaml up 

**Describe the results you received:**
 % Total    % Received % Xferd  Average Speed   Time    Time     Time  Current
                                 Dload  Upload   Total   Spent    Left  Speed
  0     0    0     0    0     0      0      0 --:--:-- --:--:-- --:--:--     0* Could not resolve host: nginx
*

**Describe the results you expected:**
client_1  | It works
nginx_1   | 172.18.0.2 - - [11/May/2022:13:06:00 +0000] "GET / HTTP/1.1" 200 615 "-" "curl/7.80.0" "-"

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
Name resolution was working under podman 4.0.3.  Podman cleaned and upgraded to 4.1.0 to use volumes.  

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.1.0
API Version:  4.1.0
Go Version:   go1.18.1
Built:        Thu May  5 16:07:47 2022
OS/Arch:      darwin/arm64

Server:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.1.0
API Version:  4.1.0
Go Version:   go1.16.15
Built:        Fri May  6 12:14:32 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/arm64
```

```

macOS Monterey 12.3.1

comments.body:
Same for me.

After an upgrade from 4.0.3 to 4.1 the containers don't see each other in the created network. 

We also use podman-compose and it worked fine before. Containers are reachable from host machine.

Can you paste the full podman commands podman-compose is using.

Could you also compare the /etc/hosts file in the container between podman 4.0 and 4.1, I reworked some parts there maybe something is missing.

I don't have a machine with podman 4.0 on it to compare :(

Full podman commands
```
$ podman-compose -f compose.yaml up
['podman', '--version', '']
using podman version: 4.1.0
** excluding:  set()
['podman', 'network', 'exists', 'downloads_default']
podman create --name=downloads_nginx_1 --label io.podman.compose.config-hash=123 --label io.podman.compose.project=downloads --label io.podman.compose.version=0.0.1 --label com.docker.compose.project=downloads --label com.docker.compose.project.working_dir=/Users/gandalf/Downloads --label com.docker.compose.project.config_files=~compose.yaml --label com.docker.compose.container-number=1 --label com.docker.compose.service=nginx --net downloads_default --network-alias nginx nginx

/etc/hosts from nginx container
```
127.0.0.1	localhost localhost.localdomain localhost4 localhost4.localdomain4
::1	localhost localhost.localdomain localhost6 localhost6.localdomain6
10.89.0.12	829f009458b2 downloads_nginx_1
```

/etc/hosts from alpine container
```
/ # cat /etc/hosts
127.0.0.1	localhost localhost.localdomain localhost4 localhost4.localdomain4
::1	localhost localhost.localdomain localhost6 localhost6.localdomain6
10.88.0.3	d51f3ece5eb8 elastic_keldysh
```

/etc/resolv.conf from alpine container
```
search lan
nameserver 192.168.127.1
```

> --network-alias nginx nginx

This should be using dns via aardvark-dns. I just checked and aardvark-dns was accidentally removed from the v4.1 machine image.

cc @baude
Is there a workaround that can be employed while the build is being fixed?
`podman machine ssh sudo rpm-ostree install -A aardvark-dns` could work I guess.
Works for me too! Thanks for the quick response. Saved my day.
